title: Ghost Transmission - Cassettes are Back!
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mNKdQ1YwHPc

Daniel (@dungeondive) explores Ghost Transmission, a solo journaling game that arrives in a cassette case—because sometimes the medium *is* the message. You're tuning into mysterious radio broadcasts, documenting strange transmissions, and piecing together an eerie narrative through prompted entries. The cassette aesthetic isn't just packaging; it's atmospheric scaffolding for the entire experience.

The game uses a deck of cards to generate prompts about what you're hearing through the static: fragments of voices, bursts of music, coded messages, and unsettling silences. Daniel appreciates how the physical format reinforces the fiction—you're handling something that feels like a found object, an artefact from some liminal broadcast space. For fans of analog horror, lo-fi aesthetics, and journaling games that commit fully to their vibe, Ghost Transmission delivers on its peculiar promise.

----

What's the most atmospheric packaging you've encountered in a tabletop game?
